summary: A neutral Alpine state at the heart of Europe, former centre of the Habsburg Empire that ruled much of the continent for centuries, now an EU member and major trade hub between Western and Eastern Europe.

history.detail: Rudolf I wins Battle on Marchfeld; Habsburgs will rule for 640 years.
Vienna besieged by Ottomans; relieved by Polish-Austrian force — turning point.
Metternich hosts; Austria dominates European order post-Napoleon.
Franz Ferdinand assassination in Sarajevo starts WWI; empire collapses 1918.
Nazi Germany annexes Austria; most Austrians initially supportive.
Occupation ends; Austria declares permanent neutrality (not NATO member).

leverage_tools: Vienna as international organization HQ (UN, OSCE, OPEC, IAEA)

resources.oil_gas: Small domestic production; gas transit hub

follow_the_money.detail: Raiffeisen and Erste have dominant positions in CEE banking markets
OMV historically major Russian gas deal holder; ongoing despite sanctions controversy

theories_disputed.detail: Austria long portrayed itself as Hitler's first victim; historians document widespread Austrian enthusiasm for Anschluss.

connections.detail: Largest trade partner; deep cultural and economic ties
Raiffeisen Bank major presence; OMV gas deals; ongoing controversy
